title: Variance & Covariance
summary: Measure spread and how two variables move together.

slides.title: Expected value
slides.content:
## Story

The **expected value** $E(X)$ is the long-run average — the "balance point" of the distribution.

**Discrete:** $E(X) = \sum x \cdot P(X=x)$

**Continuous:** $E(X) = \int x f(x)\,dx$

**Linearity:** $E(aX + b) = aE(X) + b$

slides.title: Variance
slides.content:
Variance measures **spread** around the mean:

$$\text{Var}(X) = E[(X - \mu)^2] = E(X^2) - [E(X)]^2$$

**Standard deviation:** $\sigma = \sqrt{\text{Var}(X)}$

**Scaling:** $\text{Var}(aX + b) = a^2 \text{Var}(X)$

slides.title: Covariance & correlation
slides.content:
**Covariance** (how $X$ and $Y$ move together):

$$\text{Cov}(X,Y) = E[(X - \mu_X)(Y - \mu_Y)] = E(XY) - E(X)E(Y)$$

**Correlation** (scaled to $[-1,1]$):

$$\rho = \frac{\text{Cov}(X,Y)}{\sigma_X \sigma_Y}$$

- $\rho > 0$: tend to increase together
- $\rho < 0$: one up, other down
- $\rho = 0$: no linear relationship

slides.title: Variance of sums
slides.content:
If $X$ and $Y$ are **independent**:

$$\text{Var}(X + Y) = \text{Var}(X) + \text{Var}(Y)$$

**With covariance:**

$$\text{Var}(X + Y) = \text{Var}(X) + \text{Var}(Y) + 2\text{Cov}(X,Y)$$

**Exam:** Check independence before adding variances!

slides.title: Computing $E(XY)$
slides.content:
For joint PDF $f(x,y)$:

$$E(XY) = \iint xy \cdot f(x,y)\,dx\,dy$$

**Shortcut for independent RVs:** $E(XY) = E(X) \cdot E(Y)$

workedExamples.problem: Let $X$ take values 0, 1, 2 with probabilities 0.3, 0.5, 0.2. Find $\text{Var}(X)$.
